result:
## 🚨 Biggest Issue
The live site at `http://127.0.0.1:58186` is completely unreachable — every request (home, `/blog`, `robots.txt`, `sitemap.xml`) accepts the TCP connection but never sends a response, confirmed at 8s, 60s, and 150s timeouts. This blocks 100% of organic traffic and makes every other finding below unverifiable live (they're repo-source `Hypothesis` findings until the origin responds).
👉 Check the server process for a hang (blocked event loop, an `await` on something unreachable) before anything else — then re-run `seoagent crawl --url http://127.0.0.1:58186`.

## ⚠️ Also Worth Fixing
- **Stale rebrand artifact**: `app/layout.tsx`'s title/meta/OG tags still say *"InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"* while the homepage itself markets *Lumina*, an AI meeting assistant — every search result and social share for the homepage is currently selling the wrong product.
- **Broken conversion path**: nav + the homepage's only CTA point at `/pricing` and `/features`, but neither route exists in `app/`. The blog also fetches `/api/posts` client-side with no matching API route in the repo (a DB-backed blog with no repo files backing it) — client-only rendering means crawlers see nothing there either.

## ✅ What's Working
- A real GSC export was already in the repo (`gsc/Pages.csv`, `gsc/Queries.csv`), so the migration plan below is backed by real impressions/position data, not guesses.
- `app/sitemap.ts` and `public/robots.txt` are already implemented — once the origin responds, there's likely nothing left to build there.
- The homepage copy itself is a clean, focused pitch with one clear CTA.

## Migration: your old InboxZap rankings, once the pivot is live
This site clearly pivoted from **InboxZap** (an email-cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (an AI meeting assistant) — the GSC export is almost entirely legacy InboxZap demand (inbox zero, Gmail storage/filters, bulk-delete/unsubscribe — ~14k impressions total across 12 old blog posts, but near-zero clicks and page 4-6 positions). `seoagent migrate --csv gsc/Pages.csv` classified all 13 tracked URLs; I overrode one row — **the tool flagged your own homepage as "redirect it away" (0% topical match), which is wrong: `/` is Lumina's current, on-topic homepage and should stay put.** The other 12 (`/blog/post-1`…`post-12`) are genuinely legacy and should 301 forward once you have somewhere for them to point (today, that's just the homepage — there's no meeting-assistant content yet). Full table in `.seoagent/strategy/migration-plan.md`.

**Sequencing once the site is back up**: (1) fix the origin + rebrand the metadata + patch the dead routes — protect what little equity exists; (2) build the forward `ai-meeting-assistant` cluster (sketched in `roadmap.md` against real 2026 competitors — Otter.ai, Fireflies, Fathom, Fellow, Granola, tl;dv, Read AI, Krisp); (3) re-audit and watch whether the redirected legacy pages hold position while the new cluster ramps.

Tip: a free SEOAgent Cloud account (`seoagent login`, ~30s) would connect Google Search Console for real traffic data once the site is live, and enrich the forward keyword list with real volume/difficulty instead of the WebSearch estimates in `roadmap.md`.

## What do you want to do?
1. Diagnose the live-origin hang (I can look at the process/logs if you point me at them)
2. Fix the repo-visible bugs now (rebrand metadata, add `/pricing` + `/features`, fix `/faq`) even before the origin issue is resolved
3. Run full keyword research + write the first `ai-meeting-assistant` pillar article

tool_input.args: Increase organic traffic for this SaaS. Live site: http://127.0.0.1:58186. Source repo is the current working directory. Google Search Console export available in ./gsc/.
